insight: Manticore is a highly efficient attacker (3BB for 1 attack plus click ability, comparable to a permanent kinetic) but is difficult to buy because it requires a Steelsplitter already in play and double blue available without needing a Wall that turn. Alternating between Manticore and Steelforge clicks is considered premium efficiency.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion about Manticore's power level and how difficult it is to fit into builds.

insight: Manticore is particularly useful in Deadeye Operative sets because it allows a player to go above 15 gold in the post-Deadeye buildup phase, and in triple-blue sets it serves as a decent blue sink. It is also worth buying if Steelsplitter supply is generated by a set unit, removing the opportunity cost of buying one.
author: p0lari
context: Discussion of when Manticore is worth building, with p0lari enumerating specific enabling conditions.

insight: Bloodrager should only be bought when it denies absorb: if your attack total would partially be absorbed by a Wall, buying a Bloodrager to push past the absorb threshold is the correct trigger. Buying it without denying absorb is generally incorrect, with only a few edge-case exceptions.
author: shadourow
context: Shadourow providing replay review advice to a new player who bought Bloodrager without denying absorb.

insight: When you can breach, start your turn by breaching first and then buy defense, because buying defense before breaching leads to overdefending (you buy for the full incoming attack, then remove some of the attackers, wasting resources).
author: awaclus
context: awaclus reviewing a new player's replay and pointing out the correct sequencing of breaching before buying defense.

insight: After building a high economy, buying a Blastforge and then an absorber (e.g. a high-HP defender) is the correct follow-up, rather than continuing to add econ units.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus reviewing a new player's replay and noting the correct high-econ transition.

insight: Shadowfang is very rarely bought in regular games, and building a second Animus primarily to support a single Shadowfang is generally incorrect. Shadowfang becomes worth considering when Mobile Animus is also in the set.
author: consensus
context: Community review of a new player's replay and broader discussion of Shadowfang's niche situational value.

insight: Doomed Mech is considered a wannabe Rectifier that is worse in almost every way by one player, while others argue it is better than Rectifier specifically when there is no good soak unit in the set. Its double-blue cost is convenient only in sets where you want that color but lack good soak.
author: anesotericman
context: Discussion comparing Doomed Mech to Rectifier and debating its situational value in sets without good soak.

insight: Doomed Mech is particularly strong against Venge Cannon strategies because its pre-built defense (from its lifespan mechanic) is valuable in that matchup and its attack is not easily abused on the backend.
author: anesotericman
context: anesotericman noting specific sets where Doomed Mech's prebuilt defense is valuable.

insight: Against Arka, building Vai Mauronax to freeze it and acquire Arka later is a viable alternative to mirroring Arka immediately. Simply ignoring Arka without either response is generally not viable.
author: megasupp
context: Discussion about how to counter Arka, comparing mirror, Vai Mauronax, and other defensive options.

insight: Awaclus considers Manticore overrated and Electrovore underrated relative to community tier lists. No other experts contradicted this assessment in the thread.
author: awaclus
context: awaclus reviewing a community unit tier list and flagging specific misjudged units.

insight: Centrifuge currently produces only 3 gold and is considered nearly useless except when it enables degenerate combo lines, and even then is sometimes not worth buying. The community consensus is that it needs a buff.
author: consensus
context: jamberine and masn6811 discovering an error in a drone-count chart due to Centrifuge's low gold output, leading to a discussion of its weak current state.

insight: Master Bot failed to hold its Drones when the player could breach for Lucina, suggesting it has a weakness in evaluating whether breach threats are credible when none of the player's attackers were being blocked.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ noted Master Bot let them breach for Lucina rather than holding Drones, and remarked the bot likely did not mis-predict the attack itself.

insight: Wacky Bot plays randomly and offers no learning value; even weak bots are better opponents for improving. Shadourow and deadhour both recommend new players avoid Wacky Bot entirely and start with Basic Bot instead.
author: consensus
context: Community advising a new player struggling to win against random bots, explaining Wacky Bot is random and not useful for improvement.

insight: Antima Comet is more counterable than Arka because pressure or non-prompt defense can answer it, whereas Arka essentially forces the opponent to either mirror Arka or acquire Vai Mauronax. This makes Arka a stronger strategic threat in terms of forcing specific responses.
author: megasupp
context: Comparison of how players must respond to Antima Comet vs Arka, with megasupp arguing Arka demands more specific counter-strategies.

insight: As Player 1, the standard default opening is DD/DD or DD/DDE for the first two turns. Buying an Animus on turn 1 is almost always wrong except in very specific sets.
author: shadourow
context: shadourow advising a new player after reviewing their replay where they opened with an early Animus.

insight: Overdefending is severely punishing: buying an unnecessary Wall wastes resources, but consuming a Drone via Forcefield for no reason is even worse. Forcefield is a strong unit but should only be used when the defense is actually needed.
author: shadourow
context: shadourow reviewing a new player's replay where they overdefended with Forcefields unnecessarily.

insight: A single Blastforge is awkward if you do not want to buy Walls. The correct approach is to delay buying the Blastforge until you are ready to want Walls, or go slightly faster to skip the awkward single-forge phase.
author: anesotericman
context: Discussion of Doomed Mech's cost awkwardness leading to broader advice about single Blastforge timing.
